parsed.diagnosis: This job performs exclusively small POSIX write operations of 64 bytes each, as indicated by the avg_write_size of 64 bytes and a small_io_ratio of 1.0. Such very small I/O transfers cause excessive system call overhead and metadata operations (metadata_time_ratio ~19%), severely limiting I/O bandwidth to about 29 MB/s. The sequential write pattern does not mitigate the overhead caused by the tiny transfer sizes.

recommendations.explanation: The job issues many small (64B) POSIX write calls, causing excessive syscall overhead and metadata operations. Aggregating these small writes into larger buffers of at least 1MB before issuing a write reduces syscall frequency and improves bandwidth.
recommendations.code_before: for (i=0; i<n; i++) write(fd, buf, 64);  /* 64B per call */
recommendations.expected_speedup: Up to 3-5x bandwidth improvement based on KB entries ior_ior_small_posix_t64_n4_r3_2, ior_ior_small_posix_t64_n4_r2_1, and ior_ior_small_posix_t64_n4_r2_56
recommendations.api_change: POSIX write with small sizes -> POSIX write with aggregated large buffers

parsed.overall_expected_improvement: 3x to 5x total I/O bandwidth increase by eliminating excessive syscall overhead from small writes
